articles.title: Four in five landlords unprepared for Renters’ Right Act
articles.summary: Four in five landlords (82%) are concerned about the impact of the Renters’ Rights Act, with one in two (44%) very concerned. Meanwhile, three-quarters (75%) of landlords say regulation has made them less likely to grow their portfolios, and half (49%) are planning to sell or reduce their stake within two months.
articles.ai_summary:
- 82% of landlords are worried about the impact of the Renters’ Rights Act, with nearly half (44%) expressing significant concern.  
- 75% of landlords feel regulations are deterring portfolio growth, and almost 50% plan to sell or reduce their investments within the next two months.

articles.title: Energy efficiency and EPC ratings become higher focus for tenants
articles.summary: Landlords must consider how to improve EPC ratings to attract tenants, even ahead of the new requirement to upgrade rental properties to a C rating by 2030. It comes as energy efficiency of properties has gained even greater focus as tenants look to save money after the energy price hikes caused by the Iran War.
articles.ai_summary:
- Tenants prioritize energy efficiency, prompting landlords to enhance EPC ratings to attract renters ahead of the 2030 C rating mandate.  
- The impact of rising energy costs due to the Iran War has intensified the demand for energy-efficient properties.

articles.title: Planning reform fails to inspire confidence – Landbay
articles.summary: Fewer than a quarter of the UK’s landlords feel planning reform will positively impact their business.
articles.ai_summary:
- Just 23% of UK landlords believe that recent planning reforms will benefit their businesses.  
- Landbay's findings highlight a significant lack of confidence in the government's ability to enhance the rental market through these reforms.

articles.title: Landlords warned a hidden leak could cost thousands
articles.summary: With more than half (58%) of hidden leaks originating outside a property, landlords must remember to focus on the maintenance of pipework outside as well as inside the property, leak detection specialist Aspect has warned.
articles.ai_summary:
- Over 58% of hidden leaks in properties are found outside, emphasizing the need for landlords to maintain external pipework.  
- Neglecting outside maintenance could lead to costly repairs, potentially running into thousands of pounds.

articles.title: As RRA deadline looms, 5 top tips for landlords
articles.summary: From 1 May 2026, the Renters’ Rights Act will implement some of the biggest changes to the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales. The Act will aim to balance landlord and tenant rights and will impose new requirements and regulatory oversight on landlords.
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters’ Rights Act, effective May 1, 2026, will significantly reshape the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales, enhancing tenant protections and regulatory oversight for landlords.  
- Landlords are urged to prepare for these upcoming changes by following essential tips to navigate the new legal landscape successfully.

articles.title: 84% of landlords not ready for RRA – survey
articles.summary: More than eight in ten (84%) of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act go-live on May 1, with 58% at high risk of non-compliance according to new figures.
articles.ai_summary:
- A staggering 84% of landlords lack preparedness for the upcoming Renters’ Rights Act taking effect on May 1.  
- Over half (58%) of landlords face a high risk of non-compliance as the deadline approaches.

articles.title: Expansion no longer default response to rising demand as rental growth slows
articles.summary: Landlords are restructuring portfolios and financing in response to changing rental demand, rather than focusing on expansion, with the ONS announcement of a slowing in rental growth showing the need to ensure portfolios are right.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords are shifting focus from expansion to restructuring portfolios and financing amid slowing rental growth, as highlighted by the ONS report.
- Adjusting strategies to align with changing rental demand is now a priority for property investors.

articles.title: Landlords blamed for pushing first-time and local buyers out
articles.summary: Landlords and investors have been blamed for pushing first-time buyers and local buyers out of the market, according to a new survey.
articles.ai_summary:
- A new survey reveals landlords and investors are driving first-time and local buyers out of the property market.  
- The findings highlight growing concerns about accessibility for new homeowners amid increasing competition from rental investors.

articles.title: Next government must stem landlord flow
articles.summary: Scotland's landlords have called for the next Scottish Government to formally recognise the private rented sector's (PRS) importance with a dedicated strategy.
articles.ai_summary:
- Scotland's landlords urge the next Scottish Government to acknowledge the crucial role of the private rented sector with a focused strategy.  
- A call to action emphasizes the need to stabilize the rental market and support landlord interests amidst ongoing housing challenges.

articles.title: Landlords beware of registration requirement for tax advisor
articles.summary: Conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams are among those who could fall foul of new mandatory HMRC tax advisor registration requirements from May, facing penalties of up to £10,000.
articles.ai_summary:
- Starting May, HMRC mandates tax advisor registration for property professionals, including conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams.  
- Non-compliance could lead to hefty fines of up to £10,000 for landlords and related parties.

articles.title: Licensing doesn’t lead to higher rents – council’s claim
articles.summary: The council is expecting landlords to pay £694 per property without recovering the cost
articles.ai_summary:
- A council claims that new licensing fees for landlords, set at £694 per property, won't lead to increased rent prices for tenants.  
- Landlords argue that the costs of licensing may not be recoverable, raising concerns about the financial implications for the rental market.

articles.title: Labour makes it clear – no rent controls
articles.summary: The pledge was made by Matthew Pennycook
articles.ai_summary:
- Labour's Matthew Pennycook unequivocally states that the party will not implement rent controls.  
- The announcement emphasizes Labour's focus on alternative housing solutions rather than imposing rental restrictions.

articles.title: Authority wants to stop “HMO landlord free for all”
articles.summary: The council is also drawing up detailed HMO guidelines
articles.ai_summary:
- The council is taking action to regulate the rapid growth of Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs) to prevent a chaotic landlord situation.  
- New detailed guidelines for HMOs are in the works to ensure better oversight and management in the housing sector.

articles.title: Tenants tutored on maximising Renters Rights Act powers
articles.summary: The week-long campaign begins on Monday
articles.ai_summary:
- A week-long campaign kicking off Monday aims to educate tenants on how to fully utilize their rights under the Renters Rights Act.  
- The initiative focuses on empowering renters with knowledge to enhance their rental experiences and safeguard their rights.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act provoking spike in S21 evictions
articles.summary: This is exactly what Paul Shamplina predicted
articles.ai_summary:
- The recently enacted Renters Rights Act is leading to a notable increase in Section 21 eviction notices.  
- Expert Paul Shamplina foresaw this surge, indicating potential challenges for tenants amid changing rental laws.

articles.title: Rents rising ahead of Renters Rights Act – new data
articles.summary: The data comes from the lettings agency Hamptons
articles.ai_summary:
- Rents are climbing in the UK as anticipation builds for the implementation of the Renters Rights Act.  
- New statistics from lettings agency Hamptons highlight this upward trend in rental prices.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act will kill off the hobbyist landlord – opinion
articles.summary: We're just 10 days away from the Renters Rights Act becoming law
articles.ai_summary:
- The imminent Renters Rights Act, set to become law in just 10 days, is expected to significantly impact the hobbyist landlord market.
- Experts warn that the new legislation could lead to a decline in small-scale landlords, reshaping the rental landscape in the UK.

articles.title: Gen Z entrepreneurs turn to property investment
articles.summary: Despite the red tape and extra tax poured on property investors in recent years.
articles.ai_summary:
- Gen Z entrepreneurs are increasingly diving into property investment, defying the challenges of heightened regulations and taxes.
- This trend highlights a shift in investment strategies among younger generations, showcasing their resilience and ambition in the face of adversity.

articles.title: Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group
articles.summary:
Sellers forced to cut asking prices, endure failed sales or withdraw from the market, reveals House Buyer Bureau’s Chris Hodgkinson.
The post Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Sellers are facing significant pressure in the current UK market, with many having to lower asking prices, deal with failed sales, or withdraw their properties entirely.  
- According to House Buyer Bureau's Chris Hodgkinson, the challenging conditions are prompting drastic measures from sellers.

articles.title: HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations
articles.summary:
The number of cases referred to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA) is on the rise, it has been revealed.
The post HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- HMRC is intensifying its scrutiny of property valuations, leading to an increase in cases referred to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA).
- This heightened oversight aims to ensure integrity and accuracy in property assessments across the UK.

articles.title: Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos
articles.summary:
The tenant is seen asleep in bed in the property listing photographs and video for a terrace property for sale in Penrith, Cumbria.
The post Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- A property listing for a £135k terrace home in Penrith, Cumbria inadvertently features a sleeping tenant caught on camera in the photos and video.  
- The unusual oversight has raised eyebrows and garnered attention in the housing market.

articles.title: Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers
articles.summary:
Referral fees have operated as an “unseen surcharge’ on homebuyers, according to the Conveyancing Task Force.
The post Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- The Conveyancing Task Force labels referral fees as a "hidden tax" that unfairly burdens homebuyers in the UK.
- These fees are described as an "unseen surcharge," impacting affordability in the property market.

articles.title: Lomond expands operation north of the border
articles.summary:
David Alexander, of Lomond, says Clyde Property Group will run its portfolio in the West of Scotland.
The post Lomond expands operation north of the border appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Lomond is expanding its operations into Scotland, with Clyde Property Group managing its portfolio in the West.  
- David Alexander announces the strategic move, indicating growth in the northern market.

articles.title: Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35%
articles.summary:
Geopolitical headwinds have meant that new buyer activity was lower than expected, says Foxtons’ CEO Guy Gittins.
The post Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35% appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Foxtons experiences a staggering 35% drop in sales revenue due to geopolitical challenges impacting buyer activity.  
- CEO Guy Gittins highlights lower-than-expected new buyer interest as a key factor in the decline.

articles.title: Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices
articles.summary:
Price rises have hit fuel and transport hardest but are expected to soon spread to the rest of the economy.
The post Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Rising inflation, fueled by escalating Middle East conflict, is predominantly affecting fuel and transport costs.
- Anticipated ripple effects hint at broader price increases across the UK economy soon.

articles.title: Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market
articles.summary:
The reality is that many new homes being built will never reach the market, says Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, Chief Executive of Alto.
The post Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- A staggering 90% of new homes built in the UK never hit the open sales market, according to Alto's CEO Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson.  
- This trend raises questions about market accessibility and real estate dynamics in the UK housing sector.
